title: System and method for using geological analysis for the designing of stimulation operations
abstract: Well completion (200) is accomplished by obtaining a sample of geological material (202) from the subsurface (114) and generating primary data (204) for the sample of geological material (104). The primary data include textural data, chemical data and mineralogical data. The primary data are used to derive secondary data (206) for the sample of geological material, and the primary data and the secondary data are used to generate tertiary data (208) for the sample of geological material. The tertiary data are a quantification of physical characteristics of the sample of geological material. The primary data, secondary data and tertiary data are used (210) to determine a location of a stage (150) along a well (116) and an arrangement of perforation clusters in the stage.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A method for well completion (200), wherein the method comprises: obtaining at least one sample of geological material from a subsurface (202); generating primary data for the at least one sample of geological material (204), the primary data comprising at least one of textural data, chemical data and mineralogical data; using the primary data to derive secondary data for the at least one sample of geological material (206) from the textural data, the chemical data and the mineralogical data; using the primary data and the secondary data to generate tertiary data for the at least one sample of geological material (208), the tertiary data comprising a quantification of physical characteristics of the at least one sample of geological material; and using the primary data, secondary data and tertiary data to determine a location of a stage along a well and an arrangement of perforation clusters in the stage (210). 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein obtaining the at least one sample of geological material further comprises obtaining the at least one sample of geological material from a well passing through the subsurface. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the well comprises a deviated well. 4. The method of any of claims 1 to 3, wherein obtaining the at least one sample of geological material further comprises obtaining a plurality of samples of geological material from a plurality of separate locations along a well passing through the subsurface. 5. The method of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the at least one sample of geological material comprises cuttings generated during well drilling, rock chips and rock cores obtained during well drilling or combinations thereof. 6. The method of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the at least one sample of geological material comprises particles having a size of from about 0.06mm to about 2mm. 7. The method of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein generating primary data further comprises using automated mineralogical analysis to generate the primary data. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein using automated mineralogical analysis comprises: scanning the at least one sample of geological material with a leptonic beam; and measuring at least one of a reflected lepton beam, electromagnetic radiation, secondary generated leptons and generated baryons. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein using automated mineralogical analysis further comprises: using reflected lepton beam and secondary generated leptons to generate images of sample of geological material; and using electromagnetic radiation to determine spatial deposition of elements in samples of geological material. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein using automated mineralogical analysis further comprises: using the image of the sample of geological material to determine non-mineral volumes in the sample of geological material; using spatial deposition to infer types of minerals and location of minerals in the sample of geological material; and using the types of minerals and the location of minerals to generated at least one of a spatially mapped mineralogical image of the sample of geological material and a single bulk mineral composition of the sample of geological material. 1 1 . The method of any of claims 1 to 10, wherein using the primary data to derive secondary data comprises using the primary data to generate at least one of a brittleness index for the at least one sample of geological material, a distribution of the size of the void spaces in the at least one sample of geological material, a distribution of a measure of the shape of the void spaces in the at least one sample of the geological material and a combination of both distributions. 12. The method of any of claims 1 to 1 1 , wherein using the primary data and the secondary data to generate tertiary data comprises using arithmetic combinations of at least one of primary data and second data to determine the quantification of physical characteristics of the at least one sample of geological material. 13. The method of any of claims 1 to 12, wherein using the primary data, secondary data and tertiary data to determine the location of the stage and the arrangement of perforation clusters comprises: determining a number of perforation clusters in the stage; determining a number of perforations in each perforation cluster; and determining a location of each perforation cluster in the stage. 14. The method of any of claims 1 to 13, wherein at least one sample comprises a plurality of samples of geological material for a subsurface (402), each sample of geological material obtained from a unique position along a well passing through the subsurface; generating the primary data (404), using the primary data to derive secondary data (406) and using the primary data and the secondary data to generate tertiary data (408) are performed for each sample of geological material; the method further comprising using the quantification of physical characteristics of each sample of geological material to place the plurality of samples of geological material into a plurality of groups (410), each group comprising similar physical characteristics; and 

 groups being used with the primary data, secondary data and tertiary data to determine a location of a plurality of stages along a well and an arrangement of perforation clusters in each stage (412). 15. The method of claim 14, wherein: using the primary data to derive secondary data comprises using the primary data to generate a brittleness index for each sample of geological material; and using the primary data, secondary data, tertiary data and groups to determine a location of a plurality of stages comprises segmenting the well into a plurality of lengths such that the brittleness indices for all samples of geological material within a given length comprise a lowest standard deviation from a mean value of brittleness index along the given length and the given length falls within a pre-defined maximum length and minimum length, each stage comprising one of the plurality of lengths. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the maximum length comprises about 200 feet and the minimum length comprises about 100 feet. 17. The method of claim 15 or 16, wherein using the primary data, secondary data, tertiary data and groups to determine the arrangement of perforation clusters in each stage comprises: determining a number of perforation clusters in each stage; determining a number of perforations in each perforation cluster; and determining a location of each perforation cluster in each stage. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein determining the location of each perforation cluster in each stage comprises: identifying regions along each stage where samples of geological material from a given region have similar quantifications of physical characteristics; and locating a perforation cluster at each region. 19. The method of any of claims 14 to 18, wherein: 

 generating primary data further comprises using automated mineralogical analysis to generate the primary data by scanning the sample of geological material with a leptonic beam and measuring at least one of a reflected lepton beam, electromagnetic radiation, secondary generated leptons and generated baryons; using the primary data to derive secondary data comprises using the primary data to generate at least one of a brittleness index for the sample of geological material and a distribution and shape of void spaces in the sample of geological material; and using the primary data and the secondary data to generate tertiary data comprises using arithmetic combinations of at least one of primary data and secondary data to determine the quantification of physical characteristics of the sample of geological material. 20. A system for well completion (100), wherein the system comprises: a geological sampling mechanism (102) to obtain a plurality of samples of geological material (104) for a subsurface (1 14), each sample of geological material obtained from a unique position along a well (1 16) passing through the subsurface; a primary data generating (106) unit to generate primary data for each sample of geological material, the primary data comprising at least one of textural data, chemical data and mineralogical data; a secondary data generating unit (108) to use the primary data to derive secondary data for each sample of geological material from the textural data, the chemical data and the mineralogical data; a tertiary data generating unit (1 10) to use the primary data and the secondary data to generate tertiary data for the sample of geological material, the tertiary data comprising a quantification of physical characteristics of each sample of geological material and to place the plurality of samples of geological material into a plurality of groups, each group comprising similar physical characteristics; and a well completion determination unit (1 12) to use the primary data, secondary data and tertiary data to determine a location of a plurality of stages (150) along the well (150) and an arrangement of perforation clusters in each stage.
